top_categories.workout_context: 1864 miles. Your legs will beg to quit after 120. This trains the power output you need from mile 130 onward.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Tour Aotearoa's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before February.
Tour Aotearoa's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 1864 miles.
When someone attacks on mixed terrain, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
1864 miles rewards fat oxidation. This volume work shifts your fuel mix so you're still burning fat at hour 5, not bonking at hour 3.
Long rides with hard efforts mixed in. Simulates what Tour Aotearoa's mixed terrain actually does to you.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 1864 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Tour Aotearoa, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
2-minute all-out efforts on mixed terrain. Closing gaps, responding to attacks, sprinting for position. Train the burn.
Tour Aotearoa's mixed terrain rewards steady threshold power. Surging and recovering costs more watts than just holding.
Tempo is Tour Aotearoa's race pace. The wattage you'll hold for 1864 miles on mixed terrain. Train it until it's automatic.
At Tour Aotearoa, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.

race_overlay.nutrition: Ultra-distance fueling for 1864 miles: 80–100g carbs/hour from mile 1 — don’t wait until you’re hungry. Practice your exact race-day nutrition in every long training ride. Carry backup calories. 1864 miles burns 8,000–12,000+ calories — you cannot replace them all, but you must try.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at Tour Aotearoa requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on mixed terrain to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, Race Simulation, and Gravel Specific to prepare you for 1864 miles of mixed terrain in Cape Reinga to Bluff, New Zealand.
